danger_signals.visual_form: On-screen enemy sprites and positions in real-time viewport
danger_signals.meaning: Active threat requiring immediate mech response or unit redeployment
Units becoming immobilized and vulnerable without resupply
Combat effectiveness lost; unit requires supply vehicle support

danger_signals.visual_form: Enemy units surrounding/attacking neutral/friendly outpost structures
danger_signals.meaning: Loss of resource generation and strategic position imminent

danger_signals.visual_form: Mech sprite visual degradation or altered coloring
danger_signals.meaning: Direct pilot vulnerability; loss of mech = loss of command capability

safety_signals.visual_form: Outpost structure colored/marked as player-owned; static indestructible building
safety_signals.meaning: Secure revenue generation, repair/refuel station, remote base of operations

safety_signals.visual_form: Mech sprite unmarked, full visual representation
safety_signals.meaning: Operational capacity for unit command and combat

safety_signals.visual_form: Dedicated support vehicle sprite near combat units
safety_signals.meaning: Active logistics network enabling unit sustainability

safety_signals.visual_form: Units following predictable movement patterns in designated areas
safety_signals.meaning: Tactical positioning and area denial without active micro

faction_indicators.color_identity: Likely blue or primary player color (not explicitly documented)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Enemy units display distinctly from player units through sprite palette/coloring
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Outposts change visual state (color/marking) when captured by faction; nine permanent outposts serve as faction control indicators

faction_indicators.color_identity: Contrasting color to player 1 (red or secondary palette assumed)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Distinct sprite rendering from player forces; AI opponent receives visual distinction through palette swap
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Outpost control state visually indicates enemy faction occupation; AI difficulty level increases enemy armor/damage (visual balance not communicated to player)

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Outposts display neutral state; few units visible on battlefield; sparse unit icons in command menu
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Mech rapid-deploys basic units to capture outposts; light harassment; focus on economic expansion

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Multiple outposts under each player's faction control; increasing unit density on battlefield; more varied unit types deployed
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Active skirmishing over remaining neutral outposts; patrol patterns establish; resource flow visible through unit purchase rate

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Dense concentration of units; most outposts captured; high visual saturation of sprites on screen
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Large-scale coordinated attacks; supply lines strained; constant mech micromanagement for unit orders; fuel/ammo scarcity becomes critical

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Unit sprite surrounded by enemy units; potential visual damage indicators
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Unit ceases movement orders; defensive posture assumed; mech required for extraction or reinforcement

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Supply vehicle absence in tactical area; units visually ceasing fire or movement
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Affected units immobilize due to fuel depletion; combat effectiveness cascades downward; visual stalling of unit activity

environmental_storytelling.example: Nine fixed outpost structures exist on every map; faction control is immediately visually apparent through structure coloring/marking; destruction is impossible, forcing player to contest through unit control rather than environmental destruction

Fuel and ammunition limitations are communicated through unit behavioral changes (slowing, stopping, ceasing fire) rather than UI bars; player learns resource management through observable unit degradation

Eight different vehicle types with distinct sprites represent visible trade-offs (speed vs. armor, anti-air vs. ground-attack); visual unit selection reveals player strategy to opponent in multiplayer

environmental_storytelling.visual_form: Player-controlled flying mech is always visible and must be physically present to issue orders; its visual position and damage state communicate command authority and vulnerability
environmental_storytelling.example: Mech destruction or fuel depletion immediately halts all new unit deployments; visual presence/absence tells players entire battle state
No pause menu in multiplayer; visual information updates continuously without interruption; UI and sprite movement create constant pressure conveying urgency of live strategy

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Executes assigned orders (patrol, garrison, capture base) at normal speed; maintains combat stance; visible animation
Slows movement, comes to complete stop; becomes immobilized and vulnerable; sprite may fade or visually indicate inoperability
Ceases fire animation; cannot engage enemies; maintains movement if fuel available but is defenseless
Follows units; provides refuel/rearm; visible proximity indicates logistics support
Free flight movement; can airlift units; issues commands through menu system; visible on-screen at all times
Visual degradation; continued flight capability but increased vulnerability; communicates weakness to opponent in multiplayer
Static, indestructible; visual neutral coloring; no revenue generation; available for capture
Generates visible revenue flow; acts as visible resupply/repair point; sprite displays faction coloring
Generates enemy revenue (inferred from increasing enemy unit deployment); visual enemy coloring indicates denial of resource

notes: Herzog Zwei predates mainstream RTS genre conventions (Dune II, StarCraft). Manifestation patterns rely heavily on real-time sprite behavior and absence of UI abstraction—scarcity and state are communicated through observable unit degradation rather than menu systems. The flying mech's constant visibility establishes it as world-state embodiment: its location, damage, and fuel determine all command capability. Faction identity is communicated through sprite palette swaps rather than sigils. Political/strategic communication occurs through unit deployment density and outpost control visualization in multiplayer context. No faction-specific visual language systems documented beyond color differentiation.
